content.content:
{"body":"
There is no GO term for this.
Currently it is an exact synonym of 

 GO:0006626 JSON
protein targeting to mitochondrion
Biological Process Definition (GO:0006626 GONUTS page)
The process of directing proteins towards and into the mitochondrion, usually mediated by mitochondrial proteins that recognize signals contained within the imported protein.

but targeting and import are different (targeting would be part of import, but it happens before translocation, transport is the entire process)

","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws40xMMY","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"sorry, wrong click.","createdAt":"2021-07-22T23:57:32Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-885310232","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws40zAUz","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I agree with that import and targeting should be distinct.

GO:0017038 protein import 
DEF: The targeting and directed movement of proteins into a cell or organelle. Not all import involves an initial targeting event. 

This def is bad. We need to choose either import includes targeting or not.

From the hierarchy, it seems import and targeting are distinct aspects of transport. This arrangement makes logistical sense. A cargo should have a delivery to address (targeting) and then at the destination port, goes through custom (import).
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/619520/126817422-a166f914-5d83-4dd3-a6dd-8eaa73ee60f2.png)


","createdAt":"2021-07-23T17:21:35Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-885785907","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws40zUwf","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Hi @raymond91125 you are correct, import only sometimes has a targeting step. I'm not sure that we need to choose if import includes targeting or not. It is just that _sometimes_ import includes a targeting step.

I recall some  discussions with @pgaudet  that targeting might be better represented as  MFs?  (i.e targeting would be represented as "signal sequence recognition" and any other necessary steps in the transport process).
I think this might work because 'targeting' has different meanings depending on how strict you want to be. Some people use targeting for any vesicle cargo,  and movement of ubiquitinated targets,  but I don't think that is really correct if the strict use is adhered to.

The targeting def is 
The process of targeting specific proteins to particular regions of the cell, typically membrane-bounded subcellular organelles. Usually requires an organelle specific protein sequence motif.

I remember some of the history.  When nucleocytoplasmic targeting is concerned, the NLS does not always need to be in the transported proteins, since sometimes proteins are imported  with their binding partners,  so they do not necessarily contain an intrinsic sequence.
This accounts for the clause "Usually requires an organelle specific protein sequence motif."

However, this has been misinterpreted to mean that modification dependent recognition and transport, which is not intrinsic to a protein is also targetting. I am not sure that it really  is?

Moving 'targeting' to MF would help because it could be defined as a part of a transport process. Also, we would not require different flavours of targeting  for nucleocytoplasmic transport (which is not transmembrane transport), and other types of import- people would only need to couple the activity to the transport process.

So, my preference, if possible, would be to remove references to targeting from BP, but allow targeting to be annotated as MF (what these terms would be would require discussion). It would be much cleaner....

https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/16837
https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/11908
https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/16662

What do you think? It's a bit of a mess...
","createdAt":"2021-07-23T19:36:01Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-885871215","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws42iugt","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"At some point we had said that 'targeting', at least as in 'targeting signal', was a passive process. 

@raymond91125 are you OK to tackled these? ","createdAt":"2021-09-08T09:28:54Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-915073069","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws42wcuE","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I think import and export ISA transport across a threshold, e.g. plasma, mitochrondrial, or nuclear membrane. Targeting, on the other hand, is PARTOF a transport process, that specifies the destination. We do have a another targeting term "vesicle targeting" that I think is a good example to learn from.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/619520/133168705-0cf89a6a-3c92-4ab1-8d1c-2807699294a1.png)
","createdAt":"2021-09-13T23:53:47Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-918670212","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws42wjWH","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"For protein targeting to be a MF, it must be "A molecular process that can be carried out by the action of a single macromolecular machine".  It appears that protein targeting to mitochondrion has many parts.

But the issue of targeting vs import is a bit more involved since it is not always clear when targeting ends and import begins. One solution (NEEDS A LOT OF RESTRUCTURING!) is to wrap them both into transport and do without that distinction. For example, mitochrondria protein transport has parts targeting, insertion into outer membrane, insertion into inner membrane, cleavage of targeting sequence, etc.
","createdAt":"2021-09-14T00:38:35Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-918697351","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws42xN78","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I think vesicle targeting is more problematic.  Targeting  is supposed to represent the recognition of a signal sequence,  and carrying to a location- it doesn't strictly apply to vesicles. Personally I see no difference between vesiicle transport and vesicle targeting and have wondered why we have this term.","createdAt":"2021-09-14T07:07:53Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-918871804","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws42xRLr","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Where targeting applies I like your proposed solution, with single steps as MF where possible.

It gets even weirder in the targeting to ER. 
There is a term:
GO:0006616    SRP-dependent cotranslational protein targeting to membrane, translocation
but targeting precedes translocation doesn't   it?  These 2 different concept are rolled into a single term.

The MFs for  targeting terms  would be perhaps
signal sequence recognition,
and
carrier activity

Furthermore,  for nucleocytoplasmic targeting, this is exactly what has happened. 
In 2017
2017-06-29 | Added | SECONDARY | GO:0044744 (protein targeting to nucleus)
was folded into 
GO:0034504   protein localization to nucleus
(although https://www.ebi.ac.uk/QuickGO/term/GO:0006913 (nucleocytoplasmic transport) narrow, would seem more appropriate ?)

nucleocytoplasmic transport (GO:0006913)  has
GO:0140142    nucleocytoplasmic carrier activity
as a descendant and
GO:0008139    nuclear localization sequence binding (which isn't a descendant but probably could be).

Personally I think it would be clearer to curators to model 'targeting' as functions rather than processes, targeting has always been problematic.

@pgaudet  do you agree? I think we discussed this at some point?












","createdAt":"2021-09-14T07:26:58Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-918885099","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws421M9M","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"> nucleocytoplasmic transport would seem more appropriate for GO:0034504

Is it clear to you that this is cytoplasmic-> nuclear? This is why I kept 'protein localization to nucleus' instead, to distinguish from export from nucleus. 

> GO:0008139 nuclear localization sequence binding

Maybe this is a substep of nucleocytoplasmic carrier activity and could be merged or obsoleted? 22 EXP
I suppose it could also hide NLS's, but these two concepts should not be conflated. 

> Personally I think it would be clearer to curators to model 'targeting' as functions rather than processes

I agree - although can you give an example of a protein that would have MF targeting? The signal recognition particle? The karyopherins? Is that what you have in mind? 

Thanks, Pascale
","createdAt":"2021-09-15T10:58:08Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-919916364","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws421Te-","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"> Is it clear to you that this is cytoplasmic-> nuclear? This is why I kept 'protein localization to nucleus' instead, to distinguish from export from nucleus.

Yes they do it seems
https://www.researchgate.net/publication/15157013_Nuclear_Localization_Signals_also_Mediate_the_Outward_Movement_of_Proteins_from_the_Nucleus
I didn't know that!","createdAt":"2021-09-15T11:40:33Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-919943102","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws421TyI","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"> I agree - although can you give an example of a protein that would have MF targeting? The signal recognition particle? The karyopherins? Is that what you have in mind?

Yes exactly , but would not create a 'targeting function'.
The carrier terms work just fine.




","createdAt":"2021-09-15T11:42:32Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-919944328","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws421VxT","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Ah ok - so targeting as a BP should go then. - makes sense to me. 

In particular for 'targeting', some older annotations are wrong, and were made to the 'targetee' rather than the mediator of the targeting, so perhaps obsoletion is better? (to be confirmed) 

","createdAt":"2021-09-15T11:55:21Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-919952467","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws422zi3","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Is the proposal (goal) to deprecate every term GO:0006605 protein targeting and IS_A descendents, and transfer all annotations to appropriate MF terms or respective children of  GO:0006886 intracellular protein transport (e.g. GO:0006606 protein import into nucleus)?","createdAt":"2021-09-15T20:02:48Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-920336567","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws4230Ie","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Yes - although there are about 1000 EXP annotations, so it's no that trivial. 

I suggest we start with 'regulation of protein targeting' - more than half are IMPs - see 
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1OSWsOj8gydd8_byVAtByjFs2cVlKoEXx3OCzSytVrN8/edit#gid=0

Thanks, Pascale

","createdAt":"2021-09-16T05:56:57Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-920601118","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws6le6ck","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"the subject of this ticket
protein import into mitochondrial matrix (GO:0030150) 
is dealt with here
https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29962","createdAt":"2025-04-03T16:27:41Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-2776344356","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws6lifNM","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"In the case of peroxisomal targeting of proteins, there are two steps:
Step 1: Recognition of PTS1 (Peroxisomal Targeting Signal 1) or PTS2 by cytosolic receptors (Pex5/Pex7).
Step 2: Docking at the peroxisomal membrane via the Pex14 complex.
PMID: 11457447
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/409316b8-0199-4acd-9e91-b2ef85ebf32a)

","createdAt":"2025-04-04T00:34:33Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/21852#issuecomment-2777281356","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws6lj-GL","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"> In the case of peroxisomal targeting of proteins, there are two steps:

This is the case for most targeting, there is always a receptor, and a docking.
The issue  that I solved here  https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29962 was that import was part_of targeting causing true path violations.
Targeting parts should really be the early step  in the import process, but not the other way around.

However, protein Import could be modelled more simply as the following consecutive molecular functions
cargo receptor binding (broad synonym targeting)
cargo docking (broad synonym targeting)
translocation
receptor recycling

We don't need terms like 
protein import into peroxisome matrix, docking GO:0016560
SRP-dependent cotranslational protein targeting to membrane, translocation GO:0006616 (this one especially is a real conflation of targeting and translocation)
These should be modelled as molecular functions.

This is the case for most targeting, there is always a receptor, and a docking.
The issue  that I solved here  https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29962 was that import was part_of targeting causing true path violations.
Targeting parts should really be the early step  in the import process, but not the other way around.

However, protein Import could be modelled more simply as the following consecutive molecular functions
cargo receptor binding (broad synonym targeting)
cargo docking (broad synonym targeting)
translocation
receptor recycling

We don't need terms like 
protein import into peroxisome matrix, docking GO:0016560
SRP-dependent cotranslational protein targeting to membrane, translocation GO:0006616 (this one especially is a real conflation of targeting and translocation)
These should be modelled as molecular functions.

gh issue comment 21852 --repo geneontology/go-ontology --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Analysis and Recommendations for Issue #21852

I've reviewed the full comment thread (spanning 2021-2026), the linked issues (#29962, #16837, #11908, #16662), and the current state of the ontology. Here is a summary of the discussion consensus and what remains to be done.

### What has already been resolved

1. **GO:0006626 (protein targeting to mitochondrion)** — already obsoleted, replaced_by GO:0070585 (protein localization to mitochondrion). This was the original subject of this ticket.

2. **GO:0030150 (protein import into mitochondrial matrix)** — no longer a child of "protein targeting to mitochondrion." Its `part_of` targeting relationship was removed in #29962. It now has a proper logical definition specifying transport from cytosol to mitochondrial matrix across mitochondrial membrane.

3. **Several mitochondrial import/insertion terms** (GO:0045039, GO:0045041, GO:0045040) — their `part_of protein targeting to mitochondrion` relationships were removed in #29962.

### What remains open — recommendations

The discussion converged on a broader structural proposal that goes well beyond the original mitochondrial case. Here is what I believe needs to happen, roughly ordered by priority:

#### 1. Decide the fate of GO:0006605 (protein targeting) and its children

The consensus from @ValWood, @pgaudet, and @raymond91125 was that **"protein targeting" as a BP should be obsoleted** (or at least heavily restructured), because targeting is better modeled as molecular functions (cargo receptor binding, carrier activity, signal sequence recognition) that are *part of* transport processes. 

GO:0006605 currently has **8 children via logical definitions** (targeting to membrane, lysosome, peroxisome, vacuolar membrane, chloroplast, Golgi, plus ER targeting and sub-terms). These would all need migration plans.

**Recommendation:** This is a large-scope change (~1000 EXP annotations as noted by @pgaudet). It needs a dedicated design discussion and phased plan. I'd suggest opening a new umbrella issue specifically for "obsolete protein targeting branch" if one doesn't already exist, with sub-tasks for each organelle-specific targeting term.

#### 2. Address problematic sub-step terms that conflate targeting and translocation

Two terms were specifically called out:

- **GO:0016560** (protein import into peroxisome matrix, docking) — models a sub-step of import, could be better represented as an MF
- **GO:0006616** (SRP-dependent cotranslational protein targeting to membrane, translocation) — conflates targeting and translocation in a single BP term

**Recommendation:** These should be reviewed for obsoletion or restructuring. The docking and translocation steps are better modeled as molecular functions (as @ValWood proposed: cargo receptor binding, cargo docking, translocation, receptor recycling).

#### 3. Clean up GO:0017038 (protein import) definition

The current definition reads: *"The targeting and directed movement of proteins into a cell or organelle. Not all import involves an initial targeting event."*

This is internally contradictory — it defines import as including targeting, then immediately says it doesn't always include targeting.

**Recommendation:** Revise the definition to remove the reference to targeting. Something like: *"The directed movement of proteins into a cell or organelle across one or more biological membranes."*

#### 4. Address the ER targeting branch

@ValWood noted the ER targeting branch (GO:0045047 and children, including the SRP-dependent terms GO:0006613, GO:0006614, etc.) is still tangled with transport children. Issue #16662 (SRP-dependent signal sequence recognition parentage) remains open.

**Recommendation:** This needs its own focused cleanup, probably as part of the broader protein targeting obsoletion effort.

#### 5. Start with regulation terms (as @pgaudet suggested)

@pgaudet suggested starting with "regulation of protein targeting" terms since more than half their annotations are IMPs, making them lower-risk to address first.

**Recommendation:** This is a sensible entry point for the phased approach.

### Summary

The original specific problem (mitochondrial targeting conflated with import) has been largely resolved. What remains is the broader structural question of whether to obsolete the entire `protein targeting` branch in favor of MF-based modeling. This is a significant ontology restructuring effort that would benefit from:

1. A dedicated umbrella issue with a phased plan
2. An annotation impact analysis (the ~1000 EXP annotations need migration paths)
3. Consensus on how to model targeting steps as MFs (carrier activities, signal sequence recognition, etc.)
4. Starting with the lower-risk regulation terms as a pilot

Let me know if you'd like me to proceed with any specific piece of this — for instance, fixing the GO:0017038 definition, drafting the annotation impact analysis, or creating a structured plan for the phased obsoletion.
